reviews.review_description: pretty fun, however i only have my left arm so i cant get past certain stages. Perhaps the maker of this can add a way to shoot the bow with 1 hand
I keep dying because the guy knocked my sword off the bridge and now I have no swords and can only die

developer_response.body: Sorry for the frustration! That sounds like a 'softlock' situation. We are working on a patch to prevent weapons from being lost forever. For now, please try using the 'Restart Level' option in the pause menu to reset your equipment. Thanks for your patience while we fix this!

reviews.review_title: This game does not look what it seems
reviews.review_description: Im not trying to mean but this game is misleading the pictures of shadows and blood is wrong the glore is not very bloody if this developer sees this pls make it to where it's a little better and give me a refund

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that the game did not meet your expectations.
We understand your concerns regarding the visual representation in the screenshots. Our goal is always to present the game as accurately as possible, and we appreciate you pointing this out. We are actively reviewing our store materials to ensure they better reflect the in-game experience.

Your feedback has been shared with our development team and will be taken into consideration for future updates.

Regarding refunds, Meta handles all refund requests directly, and we recommend contacting Meta Support for further assistance.

Thank you again for your honest feedback — it helps us improve

reviews.review_description: I am still getting used to VR as these are my first times using it.  I am very much enjoying it, and I get really into it but I did smash a vintage glass xmas ornament as I relieved a man of his head. 😅

developer_response.body: Whoa, that’s a wild story! Glad to hear you're getting into it, but watch out for those ornaments next time! 😄 Thanks for sharing your experience, and we’re stoked you’re having fun with it!

reviews.review_description: This game presents close combat experience in pleasant and realistic manner. Easy and fun to play.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the close combat experience. Our goal is to make it both fun and realistic, and it’s awesome that it’s resonating with you. Keep having fun, and stay tuned for more!

reviews.review_description:
I'm not sure what makes this game so addictive (I finished all the levels in one sitting); is it the solid weapons and characters? The clang of the swords? The sparks flying as metal scrapes metal? The glistening claret spray after removing a limb? I'm not sure, but I definitely had fun!

You can see gameplay and my review here (remove the space before the "be"):

https://youtu. be/63UU7wZAkRI?si=yZDb1NwC3J7Z5M__

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome feedback! We love hearing that you enjoyed the weapons, the sword clashes, and, of course, the bloody action. It’s the little things like sparks and splatter that can make a game so satisfying. Hope you continue to have fun, and we look forward to your next playthrough!

reviews.review_description: Lord Sword is another one of those games that is trying to chase the combat sim genre. This game is a hilariously epic fail at that genre BUT for some reason is still so satisfying to me. The combat is very janky and the physics are almost too much for a game like this. I had to restart the game a few times in order to play the archery level which kept glitching out. This game is very early in development but for some reason still has me wanting to play more. There are 21 levels of combat which takes you from various scenes that are fairly decent looking in scale. You are able to level up and unlock new items as you progress through the map as well. Where this game really starts to get fun is when you start chopping at body parts as they hilariously fly off and spit blood everywhere. I also liked being able to see the shield break into pieces when its taking some abuse. This game needs a ton of work but hearing the screams and the bodies flop around on the ground was just pure fun to me. Maybe im crazy but i think it has some potential in the future and would love to see more updates.

developer_response.body: Haha, it sounds like Lord Sword is definitely an adventure in itself! 😆 Sometimes the jankiness makes it even more fun, right? The body parts flying and shields breaking definitely add a ridiculous charm. Thanks for sharing your thoughts—it's clear this game has a weirdly fun potential, and hopefully, future updates will smooth things out!

reviews.review_description: The sword and shield VR game delivers an impressive experience! The graphics are stunning, the gameplay is exciting, and the sense of realism it provides completely immerses you. Using the sword and shield is genuinely challenging and fun, and battling enemies is a true test of skill. The game's detailed world and immersive atmosphere make it an unmissable experience for anyone who loves VR games. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you for the amazing feedback! We’re so glad you’re enjoying the sword and shield experience. The challenge of combat and the immersive world are what we aim for, so it’s great to hear it’s resonating with you. Thanks for the recommendation, and we hope you keep having fun with it!
